fix: always show cursor in dashboard terminal (strip TUI cursor-hide)

Copilot TUI sends ESC[?25l to hide hardware cursor. Strip it from
the WebSocket stream so xterm.js always shows a blinking block cursor
in the dashboard. Users can type and interact regardless.

commit.message:
Adaptive sub-chunking for failed API queries (#76)

* Adaptive sub-chunking for failed API queries

When a 7-day chunk fails (e.g. GitHub 502), recursively halve into
smaller chunks (7->3->1 day) instead of giving up. Successful results
at any level are merged; only genuinely unrecoverable 1-day failures
are reported as FailedChunks.

- Add Merge-ScanResult helper to eliminate duplicated merge logic
- Add -MinChunkDays and -EndDate params to Invoke-ChunkedRepoScan
- Use actual-span guard to avoid redundant recursion on short tail chunks
- Replace count-based success check with anyChunkSucceeded boolean
- Add 4 new Pester tests for sub-chunking behavior

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Address Copilot review: normalize EndDate to .Date, strengthen EndDate test assertion

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Address Copilot review round 2: clamp MinChunkDays, fix indentation, use named params, remove unused var

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

---------

commit.message:
Add automated site health check (1st & 15th of every month)

Runs on schedule + manual trigger. Creates a GitHub Issue with:
- Hugo build status + page count
- Data freshness check (10 tools — pipeline + TOML files)
- Tool count consistency (nav vs homepage vs toolkit page vs hugo.toml)
- CSS file size check (warns > 60KB)
- Console.log audit
- Action items for stale data

Sutheesh gets an email via GitHub Issue notifications — no CLI needed.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix: detect WiX bundle entries in pre-clean; delete CPython-3.14 dependency key

Root cause of 'action: Modify' mode:
- Python 3.14.2 (64-bit) GUID {0ef3959c...} is a WiX Burn bundle ARP entry
- msiexec /x returns 1605 on bundles (not an MSI product) which was excluded
  from force-deletion, so the ARP key survived every pre-clean run
- Burn finds this ARP key on next launch -> enters Modify mode
- In Modify mode BA only requests core+exe, not lib_AllUsers -> no stdlib

Fix:
- Detect bundle entries by presence of BundleVersion registry value
- Skip msiexec for bundles; directly Remove-Item the ARP key
- After registry sweep, also delete the Burn dependency provider key
  HKLM/HKCU ..\Installer\Dependencies\CPython-3.14 which is the actual
  trigger for Burn's 'bundle is installed' detection

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Implement relative positioning in runtime transition callback

The load callback in main.cpp now supports relative positioning for
auto-generated transitions. When a TransitionZone has edgeAxis set
(vertical or horizontal), the player's offset along the shared edge
is preserved and clamped to zone bounds. Legacy targetSpawn fallback
is retained for manual transitions or zones without edgeAxis.

Changes:
- main.cpp: Added pendingZone/pendingPlayerPos variables captured by
  the load callback lambda. On transition detection, zone data and
  player position are saved before starting the fade. The callback
  computes relative spawn position with bounds clamping.
- architecture.md: Updated TransitionManager description to document
  the relative positioning behavior.
- Moved completed task from ImplementationPlan.md to CompletedTasks.md.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Agent-Logs-Url: https://github.com/nelson-arbelaez-epam/todos/sessions/19af5e2b-1483-42e6-85bf-583cd2f00134

Co-authored-by: nelson-arbelaez-epam <273367797+nelson-arbelaez-epam@users.noreply.github.com>

* Initial plan

Co-authored-by: nelson-arbelaez-epam <273367797+nelson-arbelaez-epam@users.noreply.github.com>

* feat(mobile): apply ADR-0024 session store pattern with Zustand and @todos/core DTOs

Agent-Logs-Url: https://github.com/nelson-arbelaez-epam/todos/sessions/c0a58ca3-7dae-4b96-a5f4-d1dc6691e8f1

Co-authored-by: nelson-arbelaez-epam <273367797+nelson-arbelaez-epam@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix(mobile): align register flow env and test setup

* fix(mobile): simplify RegisterScreen as pure form presenter

Remove the in-screen success branch and onNavigateToLogin prop so
RegisterScreen is a pure form presenter. Post-registration redirect
is now owned by the Expo Router layer, mirroring web's Register page
pattern and ADR-0024 session-state rules.

* chore: exclude Expo auto-generated files from Biome checks

.expo/ and expo-env.d.ts are generated by the Expo CLI on every
start/build. They carry a 'do not edit' notice and must not be
reformatted by Biome.

* test(mobile): configure vitest alias for expo-router routes

* test(mobile): add unit tests for register flow components and routes

- RegisterForm: validation (empty email, short password) and submit behavior
- RegisterScreen: store wiring for isLoading, error, and register action
- Home route (/): redirect to /register when no session exists
- Register route (/register): redirect to / when already authenticated

* Require mobile API base URL

Fail fast in the mobile auth client when EXPO_PUBLIC_TODOS_API_URL is missing and align the documented default API port with the current local contract.

* Clean up MCP README boilerplate

Remove stale NestJS template reference lines and normalize the install snippet without mixing that cleanup into the mobile env-contract change.

* Pin Vitest versions across workspaces

Replace floating and divergent Vitest ranges with a single explicit workspace version so installs and lockfile refreshes stay deterministic.

---------

commit.message:
Add server-side pagination with Load More button

Backend:
- backlog GET / now accepts page/limit query params, returns
  { items, total, page, limit, hasMore } with COUNT query
- games GET / same pagination shape

Frontend:
- BacklogPage tracks page/hasMore state, appends on Load More,
  resets to page 1 on filter change or mutations
- GameLibraryPage same pagination pattern
- Added .load-more-container and .load-more-btn styles

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

feat: telemetry persistence — SQLite snapshots + TelemetrySnapshot type

Storage:
- Added telemetry_snapshots table (schema v5)
- save_telemetry_snapshot(): persist JSON snapshot per session
- load_telemetry_snapshots(): query with optional type filter

Telemetry:
- TelemetrySnapshot struct: captures rot score, drift, degradation
  stage, attention zone, tree branches, tokens used
- TelemetrySnapshot::capture(): builds snapshot from live trackers
- TelemetrySnapshot::to_json(): serializes for storage

Telemetry is now always persisted to SQLite at session end.
OTLP export to external collectors remains opt-in via
CADUCEUS_ENABLE_TELEMETRY=true.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix: add thinktradehub.com routing to Caddy

Caddy now owns ports 80/443 and routes both domains:
- paperpilotapp.com → PaperPilot frontend/backend (internal Docker network)
- thinktradehub.com → Phoenix services via host.docker.internal:
    /api/* /auth/* /health → phoenix-api:8011
    /ws/* → phoenix-ws-gateway:8031
    /* → phoenix-dashboard:3000

extra_hosts: host.docker.internal:host-gateway allows Caddy to
reach Phoenix's published host ports from inside its container.
Caddy auto-issues Let's Encrypt certs for both domains.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix: update service status widget immediately after clear GPU

After calling clear_gpu_backends(), the widget was staying stale
until the next 2-second refresh cycle. Now:

- Launcher action 2: immediately sets kobold_running/st_running=false
  and resets the refresh timer so the status bar updates instantly
- Monitor 's' key: updates service state and navigates back to
  Launcher (instead of quitting), showing cleared status
- Monitor standalone mode: draws one final frame with cleared
  state before exiting

- Exclude assets/images/ from Jekyll build via _config.yml

Build time: 118s -> 70s

commit.message:
fix: resolve 2 flaky mock integration tests

- test_cancel_running_task: increase delay to 60s, add 2s pause after
  running confirmation, accept 'failed' as valid cancel outcome (worker
  race can overwrite 'cancelled' status when container is killed)
- test_p0_runs_before_p2: occupy one concurrency slot with a blocker
  task so only 1 slot remains, forcing priority ordering to be observable
  with MAX_CONCURRENCY=2
- Use unique branch names with timestamps to avoid collisions

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Add critical: database credentials are incorrect on Render

The system is 100% built but can't function without valid DB credentials.
This file explains:
1. What's wrong (28P01 authentication error)
2. Why it matters (can't create accounts)
3. What to do (update 5 environment variables)
4. How to verify it works

Root cause: DB_USER and/or DB_PASSWORD don't match the actual PostgreSQL
service on Render. Need user to provide correct credentials.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
